title: Amauropelta rosenstockii
taxonomy: Amauropelta rosenstockii is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns. It belongs to the genus Amauropelta, a genus of ferns that are primarily found in the Southern Hemisphere. Amauropelta rosenstockii is further classified as a species within the genus. The classification of Amauropelta rosenstockii is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and venation pattern. This classification helps scientists to understand the relationships between different species of ferns.

intro: Amauropelta rosenstockii, also known as the Maori maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. The Maori maidenhair fern is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Amauropelta rosenstockii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties.
